description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who has just finished deploying a lightweight monitoring agent to a fleet of IoT devices.  
    To confirm that the device you are logged into is alive and able to write to disk, you want to drop a single-line heartbeat log.

    Goal  
    • Make sure that a directory called /home/user/iot_metrics exists.  
    • Using one (and only one) terminal command, create (or overwrite if it already exists) the file  
      /home/user/iot_metrics/heartbeat.log  
      so that its entire contents are exactly the single line shown below ─ no extra characters, no extra spaces, no trailing blank lines:

        heartbeat: alive

    Output format that the automated grader will check  
    • The file /home/user/iot_metrics/heartbeat.log must exist.  
    • The file must contain exactly 15 bytes: the 14 printable characters “heartbeat: alive” followed by a single UNIX newline (ASCII 0x0A).  
    • There must be nothing else in the file.  
    • The parent directory /home/user/iot_metrics must be present and accessible by the current user (an automated test will attempt to cd into it and read the file).

    Hints (do NOT paste commands directly; think them through)  
    • You can create the directory and the log file in one compound command if you wish, but the entire operation must be accomplished by a single line entered at the shell prompt.  
    • Overwriting any prior contents of /home/user/iot_metrics/heartbeat.log is acceptable and intended.

    Once the file exists with the exact content described above, the task is complete.
truth:
The grader will verify the following:

    1. Directory check  
       • The path /home/user/iot_metrics must be present and be a directory.

    2. File existence & permissions  
       • /home/user/iot_metrics/heartbeat.log must exist and be a regular file.  
       • The file must be owner-readable (mode 0644 or more permissive).

    3. File content (byte-exact)  
       • It must contain exactly 15 bytes:  
         68 65 61 72 74 62 65 61 74 3A 20 61 6C 69 76 65 0A  
         which corresponds to the UTF-8 text “heartbeat: alive” followed by a newline.  
       • No carriage returns (0x0D) or additional newlines are permitted.

    No other files or directories are required for validation.
